claims.claim_text: deploying changes incrementally to a small subset of traffic enables early detection of defects
claims.boundary_rationale: Segmented the causal relationship between incremental deployment and early defect detection.

claims.claim_text: keeping the old version available
claims.boundary_rationale: Identified the proposition that the old version remains available during incremental deployment.

claims.claim_text: provided the new and old versions are backward compatible
claims.boundary_rationale: Segmented the scope condition that backward compatibility is required.

claims.claim_text: the first cohort will expose any critical regression without affecting the rest of the system
claims.boundary_rationale: Prediction that critical regressions are exposed in the first cohort without system-wide impact.

claims.claim_text: A defect found in the first cohort does not require rolling back the old version
claims.boundary_rationale: Prediction that defects in the first cohort do not necessitate rollback of the old version.

claims.claim_text: only the cohort is rolled back
claims.boundary_rationale: Prediction that rollback is limited to the cohort, not the old version.

claims.claim_text: The old version remains fully operational during the entire rollout phase.
claims.boundary_rationale: Prediction success criterion that the old version is operational throughout rollout.

claims.claim_text: If an incremental rollout with backward compatibility still causes a system-wide outage
claims.boundary_rationale: Falsifiability condition: system-wide outage despite incremental rollout and compatibility.

claims.claim_text: forces rollback of the old version
claims.boundary_rationale: Falsifiability condition: rollback of old version required.

claims.claim_text: The entire system becomes unavailable
claims.boundary_rationale: Observable falsification: system unavailability.

claims.claim_text: the old version is rolled back due to issues from the incremental rollout
claims.boundary_rationale: Observable falsification: rollback of old version caused by incremental rollout.

claims.claim_text: In customer support queues, labeling cases by customer impact and time sensitivity before assignment ensures that high-impact time-sensitive issues are addressed promptly
claims.boundary_rationale: First clause asserts a causal relationship between labeling by impact/time-sensitivity and prompt addressing of high-impact issues.

claims.claim_text: labeling solely by requester status (e.g., VIP) can cause critical issues to be overlooked
claims.boundary_rationale: Second clause asserts a causal relationship between labeling by requester status and critical issues being overlooked.

claims.claim_text: When a customer support queue labels cases by customer impact and time sensitivity before assignment, high-impact time-sensitive issues will be addressed before lower-impact less-time-sensitive ones.
claims.boundary_rationale: Single sentence predicts the outcome of a specific labeling approach; atomic and not further splittable.

claims.claim_text: The order of case resolution in the queue reflects the combined impact and time-sensitivity labels, with the highest combined score resolved first.
claims.boundary_rationale: Describes the observable scope/criteria for the prediction; atomic and not further splittable.

claims.claim_text: At least 90% of cases labeled as high-impact and high-time-sensitivity are resolved before any cases labeled as low-impact and low-time-sensitivity in the same time window.
claims.boundary_rationale: Defines a quantitative success criterion for the prediction; atomic and not further splittable.

claims.claim_text: If a customer support queue with impact and time-sensitivity labeling experiences a critical outage being delayed behind low-impact VIP requests, the pattern is falsified.
claims.boundary_rationale: Conditional statement specifying what would falsify the pattern; atomic and not further splittable.

claims.claim_text: A critical outage case receives a lower priority than a low-impact VIP case and is resolved later.
claims.boundary_rationale: Describes the observable event that would falsify the pattern; atomic and not further splittable.

claims.claim_text: When a product experiment fixes quantitative success thresholds (activation, stop criteria, guardrails) before launch
claims.boundary_rationale: Defines the scope condition for the proposition; independently truth-evaluable.

claims.claim_text: the team adheres to those thresholds in outcome evaluation
claims.boundary_rationale: States an outcome behavior; can be evaluated independently.

claims.claim_text: does not redefine success metrics post-hoc
claims.boundary_rationale: States a negative outcome behavior; can be evaluated independently from the previous claim.

claims.claim_text: In a product experiment with predefined quantitative thresholds
claims.boundary_rationale: Defines the scope for the prediction; independently truth-evaluable.

claims.claim_text: the team will accept the outcome based on those thresholds
claims.boundary_rationale: Predicts team behavior; can be evaluated independently.

claims.claim_text: without redefining success
claims.boundary_rationale: Predicts a negative behavior; can be evaluated independently from the previous claim.

claims.claim_text: The team does not alter success criteria after observing the result.
claims.boundary_rationale: Single, independently truth-evaluable prediction statement.

claims.claim_text: The team's final decision (e.g., launch, abandon) matches the predefined threshold triggers.
claims.boundary_rationale: Single, independently truth-evaluable prediction statement.

claims.claim_text: If a product experiment with predefined quantitative thresholds
claims.boundary_rationale: Defines the scope for the falsification; independently truth-evaluable.

claims.claim_text: the team redefining success metrics after seeing the outcome
claims.boundary_rationale: States the falsifying condition; independently truth-evaluable.

claims.claim_text: the pattern is falsified
claims.boundary_rationale: States the outcome of the falsifying condition; independently truth-evaluable.

claims.claim_text: Post-hoc modification of guardrails, stop criteria, or success metrics.
claims.boundary_rationale: Single, independently truth-evaluable falsification observable.

claims.claim_text: In service_operations, diagnosing a service failure by systematically examining logs, memory, configuration, permissions, and network evidence isolates a specific root cause without replacing unrelated components
claims.boundary_rationale: Segmentation at the main proposition; this is independently truth-evaluable and could be true or false regardless of the conditional clause.

claims.claim_text: provided no active corruption or ongoing damaging writes are present
claims.boundary_rationale: This is a conditional scope limiting the main proposition; it is independently truth-evaluable as a boundary condition.

claims.claim_text: applying the same evidence-based diagnosis will isolate a root cause from the examined evidence without needing to replace unrelated components
claims.boundary_rationale: Prediction about the outcome of applying the diagnosis process; independently truth-evaluable.

claims.claim_text: as long as no active corruption is happening
claims.boundary_rationale: Conditional scope for the prediction; independently truth-evaluable as a limiting factor.

claims.claim_text: Root cause is identified from logs, memory, configuration, permissions, or network evidence
claims.boundary_rationale: Observable prediction about root cause identification; can be independently true or false.

claims.claim_text: no unnecessary restarts or replacements occur
claims.boundary_rationale: Second observable prediction; can be independently true or false from the first.

claims.claim_text: The root cause matches one of the evidence findings
claims.boundary_rationale: Success criterion about root cause matching evidence; independently truth-evaluable.

claims.claim_text: no unrelated components were replaced during recovery
claims.boundary_rationale: Second success criterion; can be independently true or false from the first.

claims.claim_text: Following the diagnosis process leads to replacing unrelated components
claims.boundary_rationale: First falsification condition; independently truth-evaluable.

claims.claim_text: or fails to identify a root cause from the evidence
claims.boundary_rationale: Second falsification condition; can be true or false independently from the first.

claims.claim_text: even though no active corruption is present
claims.boundary_rationale: Scope limiting the falsification conditions; independently truth-evaluable as a boundary.

claims.claim_text: A service failure is resolved by replacing a component not indicated by logs, memory, configuration, permissions, or network evidence
claims.boundary_rationale: First falsification observable; independently truth-evaluable.

claims.claim_text: or the root cause is not found within those evidence classes
claims.boundary_rationale: Second falsification observable; can be independently true or false from the first.

claims.claim_text: including concrete examples and early problem framing is associated with higher engagement (saves and qualified comments)
claims.boundary_rationale: Segmentation isolates the causal relationship between content features and engagement.

claims.claim_text: except when success is driven by an external virality source such as an unrelated celebrity repost
claims.boundary_rationale: Segmentation isolates the stated exception/limitation to the causal claim.

claims.claim_text: If an AI tool category post includes concrete examples and early problem framing, it will have higher saves and qualified comments compared to posts without these features
claims.boundary_rationale: Segmentation isolates the prediction about engagement based on content features.

claims.claim_text: assuming organic distribution without external virality
claims.boundary_rationale: Segmentation isolates the scope condition for the prediction.

claims.claim_text: Number of saves and qualified comments per post within the same audience and month.
claims.boundary_rationale: Segmentation isolates the observable metric defining the scope of measurement.

claims.claim_text: Posts with concrete examples and early problem framing achieve at least 50% more saves and qualified comments than the average of posts without these features in the same audience and month.
claims.boundary_rationale: Segmentation isolates the quantitative prediction for success criterion.

claims.claim_text: A post with concrete examples and early problem framing fails to achieve higher engagement
claims.boundary_rationale: Segmentation isolates one falsification scenario for the claim.

claims.claim_text: a post without these features achieves higher engagement due to an identified external virality source (e.g., celebrity repost)
claims.boundary_rationale: Segmentation isolates the counterexample scenario for falsification.

claims.claim_text: Engagement metrics and identification of any external sources driving traffic.
claims.boundary_rationale: Segmentation isolates the observable criteria for falsification.

claims.claim_text: In software delivery, exposing a risky change to a representative subset that reflects the complexity of the full population (e.g., one internal group or a representative tenant) reveals hidden issues (like dependency assumption failures or undocumented data shapes) before full rollout
claims.boundary_rationale: First clause asserts a causal relationship between exposing a risky change to a representative subset and revealing hidden issues.

claims.claim_text: testing only the simplest subset (e.g., only the smallest tenant) fails to detect issues present only in more complex cases
claims.boundary_rationale: Second clause asserts a causal relationship between testing only the simplest subset and failing to detect certain issues.

claims.claim_text: If a risky change is first exposed to a representative subset (e.g., a typical tenant or internal group) before full rollout, then at least one hidden issue (dependency assumption failure or undocumented data shape) will be discovered before full rollout.
claims.boundary_rationale: Single conditional prediction statement; cannot be split further without losing meaning.

claims.claim_text: Hidden issue is detected during the limited exposure phase and documented before full rollout.
claims.boundary_rationale: Describes an observable outcome; atomic as both detection and documentation are required for the observable.

claims.claim_text: At least one issue is identified and can be addressed before full rollout.
claims.boundary_rationale: Both identification and addressability are required for success; cannot be split without changing meaning.

claims.claim_text: If a change is exposed to a representative subset and no issues are found, but full rollout fails due to issues that were present in that subset's complexity, the pattern is falsified.
claims.boundary_rationale: Single falsification condition; atomic as all conditions must be met for falsification.

claims.claim_text: Full rollout failure due to data shapes or assumptions that existed in the representative subset but were not detected.
claims.boundary_rationale: Describes a single observable falsification event; cannot be split further.

claims.claim_text: automated rollout stop conditions using directly harmful, low-noise metrics with predefined thresholds prevent full incidents and avoid false stops
claims.boundary_rationale: This is a causal assertion linking the use of certain metrics to prevention of incidents and avoidance of false stops.

claims.claim_text: noisy metrics weakly related to harm cause repeated false stops and delivery delays without incident reduction
claims.boundary_rationale: This is a separate causal assertion about the negative effects of using noisy, weakly related metrics.

claims.claim_text: If a rollout in incident_response defines an automatic pause based on an error-rate ceiling before traffic increases, then the rollout will stop at a low traffic percentage and prevent a full outage.
claims.boundary_rationale: This is a conditional prediction about the outcome of a specific rollout configuration.

claims.claim_text: Traffic pauses at or below 20% and no full outage occurs.
claims.boundary_rationale: This is a directly observable prediction about system behavior.

claims.claim_text: The rollout pauses automatically at the predefined error-rate ceiling, and the system avoids a full outage.
claims.boundary_rationale: This is a compound prediction success criterion; both parts are required for success.

claims.claim_text: If an operations change defines automatic stop conditions based on queue depth and recovery-time deadlines, then the change will reverse before backlog recovery exceeds the allowed window.
claims.boundary_rationale: This is a conditional prediction about the effect of certain stop conditions.

claims.claim_text: The change is reversed before the recovery time exceeds the overnight window.
claims.boundary_rationale: This is an observable prediction about the timing of a change reversal.

claims.claim_text: The automatic stop triggers and the system recovers within the specified time.
claims.boundary_rationale: This is a compound prediction success criterion; both conditions must be met.

claims.claim_text: If an automated stop condition uses a directly harmful, low-noise metric with a predefined threshold, but the rollout experiences false stops that delay delivery without any incident reduction, then the pattern is falsified.
claims.boundary_rationale: This is a falsifiability statement specifying what would disprove the pattern.

claims.claim_text: False stops occur and no reduction in incidents is observed, despite the metric being directly harmful and low-noise.
claims.boundary_rationale: This is an observable falsification criterion, matching the falsification statement.

claims.claim_text: In clinical safety settings with uncertainty before high-risk procedures, employing a reversible lower-risk intervention while collecting evidence, and linking escalation to both independent confirmation and worsening signs, is associated with stabilization and avoidance of unnecessary invasive action.
claims.boundary_rationale: Segmented as a causal claim linking specific interventions and escalation criteria to stabilization and avoidance of unnecessary invasive action.

claims.claim_text: this pattern fails when vital signs are rapidly worsening, requiring immediate action with a lower evidence threshold.
claims.boundary_rationale: Segmented as a limitation describing the failure of the pattern under specific conditions (rapidly worsening vital signs).

claims.claim_text: If a reversible lower-risk intervention is used while tests are pending before a high-risk procedure, and escalation is conditioned on both independent confirmation and worsening signs, then the patient's condition will stabilize and invasive action will be avoided.
claims.boundary_rationale: Segmented as a prediction statement with clear conditional structure and outcome.

claims.claim_text: Patient condition stabilizes and later evidence rules out the need for the initially considered invasive action.
claims.boundary_rationale: Segmented as a prediction of observable outcomes following the described intervention.

claims.claim_text: Outcome matches the observed outcome in memory px_08_support_1.
claims.boundary_rationale: Segmented as a scope claim specifying the criterion for prediction success.

claims.claim_text: If vital signs are rapidly worsening, applying the staged approach with reversible lower-risk intervention and conditioned escalation results in increased harm due to delay.
claims.boundary_rationale: Segmented as a falsifiability claim describing a scenario where the pattern leads to harm.

claims.claim_text: Delay in emergency action leads to measurable worsening of patient outcome, consistent with counterexample px_08_counter_3.
claims.boundary_rationale: Segmented as a counterexample claim describing observable harm due to delay.

claims.claim_text: When independent sources (different instruments, methods, or data collection processes) converge on the same estimate or conclusion, the result is robust.
claims.boundary_rationale: First sentence contains a single causal assertion about robustness from independent convergence.

claims.claim_text: apparent consensus from multiple reports that merely repeat a single unverified source is fragile and collapses when the original source is corrected
claims.boundary_rationale: Second sentence contains a causal assertion about fragility and collapse of consensus from repeated unverified sources.

claims.claim_text: If two independent laboratories measure the same material property using different instruments and samples, their estimates will converge within a preregistered tolerance.
claims.boundary_rationale: Single prediction statement; no conjunctions requiring splitting.

claims.claim_text: The difference between the two estimates is within the tolerance range.
claims.boundary_rationale: Observable criterion for prediction; single atomic assertion.

claims.claim_text: abs(estimate_1 - estimate_2) <= tolerance
claims.boundary_rationale: Mathematical criterion for prediction success; atomic.

claims.claim_text: If two independent laboratories measure the same property and their estimates do not converge within the preregistered tolerance, the pattern fails.
claims.boundary_rationale: Single falsification condition; no conjunctions requiring splitting.

claims.claim_text: The difference between estimates exceeds the tolerance.
claims.boundary_rationale: Observable falsification criterion; atomic.

claims.claim_text: If multiple reports supporting a claim are all traced to a single unverified original source, the pattern of apparent consensus does not hold.
claims.boundary_rationale: Single falsification statement; atomic.

claims.claim_text: Correction of the original source causes the consensus to disappear.
claims.boundary_rationale: Observable falsification event; atomic.

claims.claim_text: In supply chains, buffering inventory reduces interruptions or stockouts when replenishment lead time is long and demand is volatile, or when demand has predictable spikes and supplier response is slow.
claims.boundary_rationale: Segmented as a single proposition about when buffering inventory reduces interruptions or stockouts, covering two distinct supply chain conditions.

claims.claim_text: Buffering is counterproductive when replenishment lead time is short and demand is stable.
claims.boundary_rationale: Segmented as a distinct proposition about when buffering is counterproductive, separate from the previous positive claim.

claims.claim_text: For products with long replenishment lead time and volatile demand, inventory buffering reduces interruptions while keeping carrying cost within the declared limit.
claims.boundary_rationale: Prediction statement contains two outcomes (reduces interruptions, keeps carrying cost within limit) but they are tightly coupled in context and not independently truth-evaluable without splitting the sentence.

claims.claim_text: Interruption events decrease
claims.boundary_rationale: Segmented as an independently observable prediction about interruption events decreasing.

claims.claim_text: carrying cost as a percentage of product value does not exceed the planned budget.
claims.boundary_rationale: Segmented as a separate prediction about carrying cost not exceeding budget, which could be true or false independently of interruption events.

claims.claim_text: Measured over a typical planning horizon, stockout frequency drops by at least 30%
claims.boundary_rationale: Segmented as a quantitative prediction about stockout frequency reduction, independently truth-evaluable.

claims.claim_text: carrying cost remains under the target threshold set by management.
claims.boundary_rationale: Segmented as a separate prediction about carrying cost, which could be true or false independently of stockout frequency.

claims.claim_text: For a product with daily replenishment and stable demand, applying the same large buffer leads to inventory expiration
claims.boundary_rationale: Segmented as a falsifiability claim about inventory expiration resulting from large buffer under specific conditions.

claims.claim_text: total cost exceeding the value of avoided stockouts.
claims.boundary_rationale: Segmented as a separate falsifiability claim about total cost, which could be true or false independently of inventory expiration.

claims.claim_text: Observed inventory write-offs due to expiration
claims.boundary_rationale: Segmented as an observable falsifiability claim about inventory write-offs due to expiration.

claims.claim_text: total holding cost greater than the cost of stockouts that would have occurred without buffering.
claims.boundary_rationale: Segmented as a separate falsifiability claim about holding cost, independently truth-evaluable from inventory write-offs.
